From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Chinese business executive and politician

In this [Chinese name](/wiki/Chinese_name "Chinese name"), the [family name](/wiki/Chinese_surname "Chinese surname") is  *[Fu](/wiki/Fu_(surname) "Fu (surname)")*.

**Fu Xishou** ([Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 傅锡寿; January 1931 – 25 March 2015) was a Chinese business executive and politician who served as [governor of Anhui](/wiki/Governor_of_Anhui "Governor of Anhui") from 1989 to 1994.

He was an alternate member of the [13th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/13th_Central_Committee_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "13th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party") and a member of the [14th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/14th_Central_Committee_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "14th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party"). He was a delegate to the [8th National People's Congress](/wiki/8th_National_People%27s_Congress "8th National People's Congress"). He was a member of the Standing Committee of the 8th [Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference](/wiki/Chinese_People%27s_Political_Consultative_Conference "Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference") and 9th Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fu_Xishou&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Fu was born in [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"),[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] in January 1931.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1)

After graduating from [Tsinghua University](/wiki/Tsinghua_University "Tsinghua University") in 1953, he was assigned to [Anshan](/wiki/Anshan "Anshan") Iron and Steel Ferrous Metallurgy Design Company (later renamed Anshan Ferrous Metallurgy Design Institute of the Ministry of Metallurgical Industry).[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1)

Beginning in September 1958, he served in several posts in the [Chongqing](/wiki/Chongqing "Chongqing") Ferrous Metallurgy Design Institute, including technician, engineer, deputy section chief of professional section, and chief engineer.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1) He joined the [Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party "Chinese Communist Party") (CCP) in August 1959.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1)

Starting in May 1966, he successively served as chief design director of chief engineer's office, production team engineer of the reform committee, chief design director of [Panzhihua](/wiki/Panzhihua "Panzhihua") Iron and Steel Base, and deputy leader and then team leader of Panzhihua Iron and Steel Base of the Chongqing Iron & Steel Design Institute.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1) He rose to become deputy chief engineer and vice president in May 1978.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1)

In August 1982, he was promoted to become president and [party secretary](/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party_Committee_Secretary "Chinese Communist Party Committee Secretary") of the [Ma'anshan](/wiki/Ma%27anshan "Ma'anshan") Iron and Steel Design and Research Institute, and served until June 1987.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1) He also served as [deputy party secretary](/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party_Deputy_Committee_Secretary "Chinese Communist Party Deputy Committee Secretary") of Ma'anshan from February 1985 to June 1987.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1)

In April 1989, he became [governor of Anhui](/wiki/Governor_of_Anhui "Governor of Anhui"), a post he kept until December 1994.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1) He concurrently served as deputy party secretary from June 1987 to January 1995. He retired in March 2004.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1)

On 25 March 2015, he died from an illness in [Hefei](/wiki/Hefei "Hefei"), [Anhui](/wiki/Anhui "Anhui"), at the age of 84.[[1]](#cite_note-xin-1)
